refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (1) performance targets are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (2) baseline is recorded with timestamp, operator ID, and mission context; (3) history log captures all updates with reason codes and approval chain; (4) traceability from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline record is unbroken; (5) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety constraints)
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record performance baseline with full context (timestamp, operator, mission parameters, safety margins)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes
CR-NGHE-005.4: Provide traceability from worksite input to derived requirement to baseline record
CR-NGHE-005.5: Enforce audit trail and approval workflow for requirement changes (safety-critical)

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – a rule-based inference system that ingests worksite mission parameters (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety policy) and outputs derived performance targets; stores baselines in a versioned database with full audit trail and change management workflow.

DP-B: Distributed Performance Calculation – embed performance derivation logic in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules; each module calculates its own performance targets and reports to a central registry; history managed by a lightweight log aggregator.

DP-C: Manual Performance Specification with Template Library – operators select from pre-defined performance templates based on mission type; templates are stored with version history; changes require manual approval and logging.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Validation Loop – automatic derivation engine generates candidate targets; Tele-operator or Site Manager validates and approves; approved targets become baseline; all versions retained in audit log.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Validation Loop – automatic derivation engine generates candidate performance targets from worksite mission context using rule-based inference; generated targets are presented to authorized operator (Tele-operator or Site Manager) for validation and approval; approved targets are recorded as baseline with full context (timestamp, approver ID, mission parameters, safety margins); all versions and change reasons retained in immutable audit log with change approval workflow.

potential_sources: DP-D depends on accurate worksite mission context input (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety policy) – must be decoupled from specific Vehicle or Teleoperation implementation; interface via Tasking domain.
DP-D depends on operator approval authority – must be decoupled from specific operator role; interface via Telemetry or SafetyAlert for approval signaling.

DP-D generates performance targets that feed downstream design decisions (e.g., Vehicle motion planning, Teleoperation bandwidth allocation) – must be decoupled via published baseline record, not direct coupling.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D is weakly coupled to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules because it consumes mission context (not vehicle state) and publishes baseline records (not real-time commands). Validation loop introduces human-in-the-loop, which reduces algorithmic coupling but adds operational dependency on operator availability. Acceptable for high-safety-criticality requirement.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety policy) is available in structured, machine-readable form at system startup or mission planning time.
ASSUMPTION: Authorized operators (Tele-operator, Site Manager) are available and trained to validate derived performance targets within acceptable time window (e.g., <5 min for mission planning).
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation rules (inference engine) are stable and validated against historical mission data and safety standards.
RISK: If worksite mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived targets may be invalid; mitigation: require explicit validation and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: If operator approval is delayed or unavailable, mission planning is blocked; mitigation: implement timeout with escalation to Safety Manager.
RISK: Audit log may grow unbounded; mitigation: implement retention policy and archival strategy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-D is sound. Recommend explicit specification of: (1) inference rule set and validation criteria, (2) operator approval authority and timeout policy, (3) audit log schema and retention policy, (4) fallback behavior if derivation fails or operator rejects targets.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the end-to-end performance requirement derivation, baseline recording, and history management system. It integrates mission context inputs from worksite planning, orchestrates the derivation engine, manages operator approval workflow, and maintains the authoritative baseline and audit log. The module provides performance targets as a published service to downstream consumers (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, operational policies, safety constraints) from Site Manager or mission planning system. Used as input to performance derivation engine.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator approval/rejection signals for derived performance targets (in); publishes approved baseline record and performance targets to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules (out). Also receives historical performance data from Vehicle for calibration and trend analysis.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Publishes safety-critical performance targets (e.g., safety margins, availability thresholds) to Safety Services for real-time monitoring and enforcement. Alerts if derived targets violate safety policy.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for distributed approval workflow (e.g., Site Manager approves from remote office) and audit log synchronization across NGHE Solution instances.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of system-level performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit trail. The requirement is explicitly about the 'NGHE solution' providing a method. Derivation engine, baseline database, and audit log are cohesive and belong in a single module. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces are Tasking (input), Telemetry (approval signaling), SafetyAlert (safety targets), and Network Link (distributed approval). No duplication across Vehicle or Teleoperation.

Vehicle module could theoretically calculate its own performance targets (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency based on vehicle dynamics), but this would fragment the requirement derivation logic and create inconsistency with Teleoperation targets. Vehicle should consume published baseline from NGHE Solution, not own derivation. High diffusion risk if performance logic is scattered across Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Teleoperation module could theoretically calculate bandwidth or latency performance targets, but this would fragment the requirement derivation logic. Teleoperation should consume published baseline from NGHE Solution. High diffusion risk if performance logic is scattered. Teleoperation may provide input (e.g., operator constraints) to NGHE Solution derivation, but should not own the derivation itself.

Safety Services could theoretically validate derived performance targets against safety policy, but the derivation itself (mapping mission context to targets) is not a Safety Services responsibility. Safety Services should consume safety-critical targets from NGHE Solution via SafetyAlert interface and enforce them. Ownership of derivation in Safety Services would create coupling and diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the single authoritative owner of performance requirement derivation, baseline recording, and history management. This module integrates mission context (Tasking), orchestrates operator approval (Telemetry), publishes safety-critical targets (SafetyAlert), and maintains audit trail (Network Link). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers of the published baseline, not owners of derivation logic.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module is not yet fully defined or has unclear boundaries, ownership assignment may be premature. Mitigation: confirm NGHE Solution module charter and interface contracts before detailed design.

RISK: Operator approval workflow (Telemetry interface) may introduce latency or availability issues if operators are not always available. Mitigation: implement timeout and escalation policy; document in operational procedures.
RISK: Audit log may become a performance bottleneck if not properly indexed and archived. Mitigation: implement database design review and performance testing.

RISK: If worksite mission context (Tasking input) is not standardized, derivation engine may fail or produce inconsistent results. Mitigation: define Tasking interface schema and validation rules explicitly.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ownership assignment to NGHE Solution is sound. Recommend: (1) explicit definition of NGHE Solution module charter and interface contracts, (2) detailed specification of Tasking input schema (mission context structure), (3) operator approval workflow and timeout policy, (4) audit log schema, retention policy, and performance requirements, (5) fallback behavior if derivation fails or operator rejects targets.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FR CR-NGHE-005 clearly specifies what the system must do: derive, record, and manage performance requirements from worksite mission context with full traceability and audit trail. Selected DP-D (Hybrid Derivation with Validation Loop) directly addresses this: automatic derivation engine + operator validation + baseline recording + audit log. The mapping is clear and non-ambiguous. DP-D is implementable and testable. No functional gap identified.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): If performance derivation logic is not centralized in NGHE Solution, it may be duplicated in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: enforce single-source-of-truth principle; Vehicle and Teleoperation consume published baseline, not derive independently.

DIFFUSION RISK (LOW): If audit log is not centralized, approval workflow may be fragmented across modules. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns audit log; all changes flow through NGHE Solution approval workflow.

DIFFUSION RISK (MEDIUM): If Tasking interface is not well-defined, mission context may be interpreted differently by different modules. Mitigation: define Tasking schema explicitly; validate at NGHE Solution boundary.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the natural and only appropriate owner of performance requirement derivation and baseline management. Containment score of 95 reflects strong cohesion and minimal diffusion. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and do not leak derivation logic to other modules. Ownership is clear and unambiguous.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible via NGHE Solution module testing: (1) unit tests of derivation engine against known mission contexts and expected targets, (2) integration tests of baseline recording and audit log, (3) system tests of operator approval workflow and traceability, (4) acceptance tests against worksite mission data and historical baselines. All verification activities are contained within NGHE Solution module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) are all allocated to NGHE Solution. No ambiguity or split ownership. Interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are via published baseline (Telemetry, SafetyAlert), not via shared ownership of derivation logic.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Explicitly define Tasking interface schema for mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety policy). Include validation rules and error handling.
REVISION 2: Specify operator approval workflow in detail: who can approve (roles), timeout policy, escalation path, and audit trail for approval decisions.
REVISION 3: Define audit log schema, retention policy, and archival strategy. Include performance requirements (e.g., query latency, storage capacity).
REVISION 4: Specify fallback behavior if derivation engine fails (e.g., use conservative defaults, alert operator, block mission planning).
REVISION 5: Define performance targets for NGHE Solution module itself (e.g., derivation latency <5 min, baseline recording latency <1 sec, audit log query latency <100 ms).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is well-formed, non-atomic, and has clear sub-FRs. DP-D (Hybrid Derivation with Validation Loop) is sound and addresses the refined FR without coupling issues. NGHE Solution is the clear and appropriate owner with high containment score (95) and low diffusion risk. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. The requirement is ready for decomposition to the next level. Sub-FRs are specific enough to guide detailed design and are traceable to acceptance criteria (documented performance targets, managed baselines/calibration updates).
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets from worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety constraints)
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record performance baseline with full context (timestamp, operator, mission parameters, safety margins)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes
CR-NGHE-005.4: Provide traceability from worksite input to derived requirement to baseline record
CR-NGHE-005.5: Enforce audit trail and approval workflow for requirement changes (safety-critical)

assumptions: Worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety policy) is available in structured, machine-readable form at system startup or mission planning time.
Authorized operators (Tele-operator, Site Manager) are available and trained to validate derived performance targets within acceptable time window (e.g., <5 min for mission planning).
Performance derivation rules (inference engine) are stable and validated against historical mission data and safety standards.
NGHE Solution module is fully defined with clear charter and interface contracts.
Tasking interface provides mission context in a standardized schema.
Telemetry interface supports operator approval signaling and baseline publication.
SafetyAlert interface supports safety-critical target publication.
Network Link supports distributed approval workflow and audit log synchronization.
open_risks: If worksite mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived targets may be invalid. Mitigation: require explicit validation and fallback to conservative defaults.
If operator approval is delayed or unavailable, mission planning is blocked. Mitigation: implement timeout with escalation to Safety Manager.
If Tasking interface is not well-defined, mission context may be interpreted differently by different modules. Mitigation: define Tasking schema explicitly; validate at NGHE Solution boundary.
If performance derivation logic is not centralized, it may be duplicated in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: enforce single-source-of-truth principle.
Audit log may grow unbounded or become a performance bottleneck. Mitigation: implement retention policy, archival strategy, and performance testing.
High safety criticality requires robust validation and approval workflow. Mitigation: implement formal approval process with audit trail and escalation policy.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of Tasking interface schema for mission context (terrain, payload, cycle count, safety policy).
Specification of performance derivation rules and inference engine logic.
Operator approval workflow specification (roles, timeout, escalation, audit trail).
Audit log schema, retention policy, and archival strategy.
Fallback behavior specification if derivation fails or operator rejects targets.
Performance requirements for NGHE Solution module (derivation latency, baseline recording latency, audit log query latency).
Validation plan for derivation engine against historical mission data and safety standards.
Test plan for operator approval workflow and traceability verification.
Safety analysis for high-criticality requirement (e.g., FMEA, fault tree).
